title: aquilegia vulgaris var. longisepala
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris var. longisepala, also known as the long-spurred columbine, is a variety of flowering plant in the buttercup family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The long-spurred columbine is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
